_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Identify, assess, and recommend cybersecurity or cybersecurity-enabled products for use within a system and ensure recommended products are in compliance with organization's evaluation and validation requirements.
reference_document_element_description: Ability to evaluate and adopt new technologies, frameworks, and tools for data and AI work, assessing maturity, community support, integration requirements, and organizational fit before recommending adoption.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI TF domain. TF-A-003 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RC-A-005 (RC, cosine: 0.507), OP-T-008 (OP, cosine: 0.497).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.4/10 (raw 0.537). Shared vocabulary: [requirements]. DCWF-only terms [assess, compliance, cybersecurity, enabled, ensure, evaluation] vs WIDAI-only terms [ability, adopt, adoption, ai, assessing, community] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.884 (P=0.885, R=0.883). Balanced P/R — the 26-word DCWF element and 27-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). "Identify, assess, and recommend cybersecurity or cybersecurity-enabled products" and "Ability to evaluate and adopt new technologies, frameworks, and tools for data a" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.530 ≈ STS 0.537 (gap 0.007). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [requirements] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 6.7%. Shared tokens: [requirements]. DCWF-only: [assess, compliance, cybersecurity, enabled, ensure, evaluation]. WIDAI-only: [ability, adopt, adoption, ai, assessing, community].
